---
title: "Coding Projects"
toc: true
toc-title: "Projects"
---

A collection of personal and professional coding projects spanning machine learning, quantitative finance, and data science.

---

::: {.project-card}
## ML Algorithms from Scratch

**Tags:** `Python` `Machine Learning` `NumPy` `pandas`

Three machine learning algorithms implemented from scratch — no scikit-learn, no deep learning frameworks. Each algorithm supports both classification and regression and is benchmarked on six UCI datasets: Breast Cancer Wisconsin, Car Evaluation, House Votes 1984, Abalone, Forest Fires, and Machine CPU.

**Algorithms implemented:**

- **k-Nearest Neighbors** — Minkowski distance with configurable exponent, Value Distance Metric (VDM) for nominal attributes, Gaussian kernel-weighted regression, and a training set editing/condensing algorithm
- **Decision Tree (CART)** — Information gain ratio splitting for classification, MSE-minimizing splits for regression, and cost-complexity pruning on a held-out tuning set
- **Feedforward Neural Network** — Sigmoid activations, full backpropagation with the delta rule, softmax + cross-entropy loss for classification, MSE for regression, and optional autoencoder pre-training for unsupervised feature learning

[View Code](https://github.com/dicer417/ml-algorithm-comparison){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.project-card}
## RL Algorithms from Scratch

**Tags:** `Python` `Reinforcement Learning` `NumPy` `pandas`

Three tabular reinforcement learning algorithms implemented from scratch — no RL frameworks, no external solvers. Each algorithm learns to navigate a custom racetrack environment with non-deterministic physics (20% chance of acceleration failure per step) across four track shapes and two crash-recovery behaviors.

**Algorithms implemented:**

- **Q-Learning** — Off-policy TD control; learns optimal Q-values via greedy bootstrapping with ε-annealing
- **SARSA** — On-policy TD control; updates Q-values using the actual next action taken, yielding more conservative policies
- **Value Iteration** — Model-based dynamic programming; iterates Bellman optimality equations to convergence, explicitly modeling both deterministic and stochastic transitions

[View Code](https://github.com/dicer417/reinforcement-learning-algorithm-comparison){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary}
:::

<!--
HOW TO ADD A PROJECT
=====================
Copy the template block below and fill in your details.
For Shiny apps, embed them with an <iframe> pointing to your shinyapps.io URL:

::: {.project-card}
## Project Title

**Tags:** `R` `Shiny` `Finance`

Brief description of what the project does and why it's interesting.

[View Code](https://github.com/yourusername/repo){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary .me-1}
[Live Demo](https://yourusername.shinyapps.io/app-name/){.btn .btn-sm .btn-primary}

<details>
<summary>Preview</summary>
<iframe src="https://yourusername.shinyapps.io/app-name/" width="100%" height="600px" style="border:1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius:4px;"></iframe>
</details>
:::
-->
